THE STAR LEAGUE ERA The Hegemony engaged in its share of battles over the next century but equally often served as mediator between warring parties Despite the Hegemony'$ history of  military expansion Terra presence at its heart Bave certain credi bility as peacemaker. Ian Cameron, who became director gen: eral in 2549, expanded the Hegemony'$ peacemaking role and negotiated an end t0 number of conflicts In 2556 Ian per- suaded the leaders of the Free Worlds League ad the Capellan Confederation t0 sign the Treaty of Geneva; this famous docu- ment Iaid the groundwork for the formation of the Star League_ the   interstellar alliance that all too briefly  ended wars and advanced the   general welfare humanity The Lyran Commonwealth signed the treaty in 2558; the Federated Suns_ 2567 With the inclusion of the Draconis Combine in 2569 Ian Cameron   achieved his dream uniting vir tually   all humanity under one leader: Led by the enlightened Cameron dynasty, the Star League gave its citizens peace and prosperity for nearly two hundred years. Though even the Star League could not wipe out the human need for conflict, kept disputes between Its member- states under firm control. After Lord Simon Cameron" tragic death in 2751 the rulers of all the member-states served as regents for Simon'$ young son Richard Cameron but unfortu nately abused their positions to jockey for personal power; The lonely Richard turned Stefan Amaris; ruler of the Rim Worlds Republic the  far-off Periphery, triendship and advice . Amaris hated the Camerons ad used his false friendship with Richard destroy the Star League trom within. December   2766. Stefan Amaris   murdered Richard and took control of the Star League within weeks Inis coup d'etat;  General Aleksandr Kerensky; commander the Star League Defense Force launched bitter, thirteen-year war t0 liberate the League from the usurper' grasp. On 29 September 2779_ Kerensky led the assault against Amaris last stronghold on Terra; the face of overwhelming torce _ Amaris surrendered Kerensky ordered SLDF troops t0 summarily execute him , his family and his clos- est aides tor their crimes against numanity. In  Iate  2780 the power-hungry Council Lords stripped Kerensky of his title as Protector of the Realm and ordered him to disperse all SLDF units to their peacetime locations_ Bereft ol  centra leadership the member states of the Star League vied with one another for control of the League Unable t0 agree on which of them snould become the Jet First Lord the ords officially dissolved the High Council in August of 2781, left Terra and began build their  power bases When the various lords attempted to persuade SLDF units to back their bids for power, General Kerensky took drastic action_ On 14 February 2784 Kerensky proposed to his troops that the SLDF should eave the Inner Sphere and found new society based on the ideals of the now-defunct Star League_ In Iate November 2784_
more than 80 percent of the SLDF departed with Kerensky' $ Operation Exodus The bewildered people of the Inner Sphere, mourning the loss of their hero comforted themselves with the belief that Kerensky and his people would return when humani: needed them:
CENTURIES OF WAR In the resulting power vacuum, the rulers of the realms now called the Successor States fought endless_ brutal wars each seeking t0 L~establish the Star League under his own leader- ship. Throughout three hundred years of conflict; the Successor Lords accomplished Iittle save t0 blast humankind virtually back to the Stone Age_ By the end of the third so called Succession War, humanity had lost nearly every technological advance that the Star League had made possible_ Only stringent restrictions destroying JumpShips and other irreplaceable technologies allowed  interstellar combat continue _ The   fighting ground down t0 endless border skirmishes in which no combat" ant could Rain significant advantage. One of the few technologies that survived was the hyper pulse generators that made interstellar communication possi: ble. These were jealously Guarded by the Holy Order of ComStar, founded by Star League Minister of Communications Jerome Blake in order to preserve communications during the coming years of war: The Organization gradually evolved into cult that worshiped Blake'$   writings and dedicated itself guarding technology' = secrets:
STEPS TOWARD PEACE the turn of the thirty-first   century; common wisdom among Successor State militaries held that conquest of Inner Sphere   through conventional war fare was impossible_ Those who wished to found second Star League had to find another way; 3020, Archon Katrina Steiner of the Lyran Commonwealth sent peace proposal to her fellow Successor Lords_ but only Prince Hanse Davion of the Federated Suns showed any interest: In 3022, the Archon and the prince con: cluded secret allliance that would bind their realms together through Hanse Davion's marriage Katrina" daugnter and heir, Melissa Steiner This union joined two families ad two nations into single strong realm, combining the prosperous Lyran Commonwealth with the militarily powertui Federated Suns_ also put the Draconis Combine in a uncomfortable position between two of its greatest enemies and led the small: Capellan Confederation and Free Worlds League to fear con: quest by the emerging Federated Commonwealth; After months secret negotiations among the Capellan Confederation; the Draconis Combine and tne Free   Worids League three signed the Concord of Kapetyn  in 3024_ Intended as counter to the Federated Commonwealth, this triple alliance provided for mutual support and defense also guaranteed that any renewed war would engulf the entire Inner Sphere_ The first decades of the new century also saw the begin: nings of technological renaissance set in motion by the unex-
